name: Sourdough Starter W/Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the water into a crock or a wide mouth gallon jar.
2. Pour in the yeast and let it dissolve.
3. Stir, then add vinegar, sugar, and flour.
4. Mix well, cover with cloth, and set in a warm place to sour for 2-3 days.
5. When activity stops and the mixture flattens out, an amber-colored liquor forms on top, and it smells right, mix everything together until it looks like whipping cream.
6. Transfer the starter to a glass jar with a screw lid and refrigerate.
7. It will keep for months and grow better as it ages slowly.
Growing this starter allows you to make pancakes; waffles; coffee cake; quick drop biscuits; cinnamon rolls; cobbler; bread; cake; oatmeal cookies, etc. Experiment and enjoy the tasty results!
